plugin.name: MCP Adapter
plugin.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) router: connect to MCP servers and publish their tools as N.E.K.O plugin entries
panel.subtitle: Manage MCP Server connections, discover tools, and publish external capabilities as N.E.K.O plugin entries.
panel.errors.addServerFormUnavailable: The current context does not expose add_server, so the generated form cannot be used.
panel.filter.title: Filter
panel.filter.label: Search servers, tools, and entries
panel.filter.help: Filtering is debounced so typing stays responsive.
panel.filter.placeholder: Search by server, transport, tool, entry...
panel.alerts.serverErrors: Some MCP Servers are unhealthy. Check the Error column below and reconnect them if needed.
panel.gateway.title: Gateway Status
panel.flow.title: Setup Flow
panel.flow.addServer.title: Add Server
panel.flow.addServer.body: Save an MCP Server configuration with the form or JSON import.
panel.flow.connect.title: Connect and Discover Tools
panel.flow.connect.body: After connection succeeds, the adapter discovers tools and registers them as dynamic plugin entries.
panel.flow.invoke.title: Invoke from Entries
panel.flow.invoke.body: Discovered tools appear in the plugin entries list and can be used by workflows or other plugins.
panel.servers.selectionHint: Click a table row to select a Server, then connect, disconnect, or remove it.
panel.servers.empty.title: No MCP Server
panel.servers.empty.description: Use the form below or JSON import to add your first MCP Server.
panel.servers.removeConfirmMessage: Remove {count} selected MCP Server(s)? This cannot be undone.
panel.addServer.title: Add MCP Server
panel.form.name: Server Name
panel.import.title: Import from JSON
panel.import.description: Paste a single server, a server map, or a Claude Desktop / Cursor mcpServers config.
panel.import.noServers: No valid server configuration was found
panel.transport.title: Transport Selection
panel.transport.stdio: Local MCP Server launched by a command such as uvx or npx
panel.transport.streamableHttp: Remote service with streamable HTTP support
panel.transport.securityDescription: Do not import untrusted commands or environment variables
panel.entries.title: Published Plugin Entries
panel.entries.columns.name: Name
panel.entries.tip: After an MCP Server connects, discovered tools are dynamically registered as entries and shown here.
panel.warnings.stdio: stdio transport starts a local process. Verify the command source before importing configuration to avoid running an untrusted MCP Server.
quickstart.title: MCP Adapter Quickstart
quickstart.subtitle: Connect an MCP Server from zero to one: understand the purpose, choose transport, save config, and discover tools.
quickstart.cards.connect.title: 1. Connect External Capabilities
quickstart.cards.connect.body: The adapter connects MCP Servers that use stdio, SSE, or streamable-http transport.
quickstart.cards.discover.title: 2. Discover Tools
quickstart.cards.discover.body: After connection succeeds, it reads the server's exposed tools, parameter schemas, and descriptions.
quickstart.cards.publish.title: 3. Publish Entries
quickstart.cards.publish.body: Each MCP tool is adapted into a dynamic N.E.K.O plugin entry.
quickstart.alert.configured: You already have MCP Servers configured. Go to the MCP Adapter panel to connect, disconnect, remove, or import more Servers.
quickstart.alert.empty: No MCP Server is configured yet. Read the steps below, then add your first Server in the MCP Adapter panel.
quickstart.path.title: Recommended Setup Path
quickstart.path.serverType.title: Confirm MCP Server Type
quickstart.path.serverType.body: Use stdio for local commands and prefer streamable-http for remote services. Older services may still use SSE.
quickstart.path.prepare.title: Prepare Configuration
quickstart.path.prepare.body: Choose a stable server name, fill in the launch command or remote URL, and decide whether to auto-connect.
quickstart.path.add.title: Add Server in the Panel
quickstart.path.add.body: Open the MCP Adapter panel and save the configuration with the form or JSON import.
quickstart.path.verify.title: Check Tool Discovery
quickstart.path.verify.body: After connection succeeds, confirm that tools are registered in the Published Plugin Entries section.
quickstart.path.troubleshoot.title: Troubleshoot Errors
quickstart.path.troubleshoot.body: If connection fails, check the Error column in the panel table and inspect detailed terminal output in Logs.
quickstart.jsonImport.title: JSON Import Example
quickstart.jsonImport.body: If you copy MCP configuration from another tool, paste a similar structure into the panel's JSON import area.
quickstart.fields.title: Key Fields
quickstart.fields.name: Unique Server identifier used for later connect and disconnect actions
quickstart.fields.command: Launch command for stdio mode, for example uvx
quickstart.fields.args: Command argument array for stdio mode
quickstart.fields.env: Environment variables injected into the local process
quickstart.faq.title: FAQ
quickstart.faq.noEntries: No entries after connection: confirm the server is connected and tools_count is greater than 0.
quickstart.faq.stdioFailed: stdio launch failed: confirm the command is executable in the current environment and paths exist.
quickstart.faq.remoteFailed: Remote connection failed: confirm URL, network access, and MCP protocol version.
quickstart.next.title: Next Steps
quickstart.next.body: This guide only provides explanation and configuration references. All connection, import, and removal actions happen in the MCP Adapter panel.
quickstart.warning.stdio: Do not import stdio configuration from unknown sources. stdio transport starts a local process, so commands, arguments, and environment variables must come from trusted sources.
